video_title: Tyler Perry’s She The People | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Before seeing the title of this show I noticed certain characters featured in trailer.  It's called She the People but all I saw was Sheeple (Sheeople) or sheep people aka walking zombies.  When you get rid of the invisibility of the 4th wall and make it solid this confuses the actor, and they know it.  Tyler Perry Studios is the size of a neighborhood, and the sets resemble homes over stages meant for plays.  Now imagine being a child when you were thrust into the world of acting.  Imagination brought to life, and those meant to protect you, throw you to the wolves.  Go on set for an audition and never leave is the deal of the day and they make a lot of daily deals.  We literally live in a country when weed in black communities or driving while black is a bigger worry to law enforcement than the millions of women, children and even men who disappear each year.  Priorities all messed up but no outrage from the people, so they continue with their ignorance.  Only god can save USAll now.

comment_text: I am not a fan of the majority of Tyler Perry's films with the exception of Family That Preys, starring the iconic Alfre Woodard, Kathy Bates, and my girl Sanaa Latham.  Alex Cross, starring Tyler Perry. I can do bad all by myself featuring the iconic. Gladys Knight, and Mary j. Blige, starring Taraji p. Henson. Otherwise, I'm not a fan of any of Tyler Perry's, sitcoms, or the majority of his movies. There's only so much you can take of his most famous character and role as Madea,. But let me be clear that anything that the late great Queen Cecily Tyson starred in was worth taking a peek at simply for her. This new film, I will watch because of the starring lead role actress, Terri j. Vaughn. It's nice to see her in a starring role where she can be viewed as an articulate smart accomplished black woman, rather than a ghetto spicy side chick, which has been a typecast for her over the last few decades. We'll see......
